summary: Crisp Jerry tone fuels a soaring Dark Star/MLB marathon
blurb: The second set erupts with a monumental Dark Star → Playing In The Band jam, followed by an inspired Morning Dew and a soaring encore of Eyes Of The World and Stella Blue. Jerry’s guitar is crisp and front‑center while Phil delivers a standout solo in Dark Star; the early first‑set mix is keyboard‑heavy but tightens after Don’t Ease Me In.
review: On October 19, 1973 at the Oklahoma City Fairgrounds Arena the Dead delivered a show that rewards repeated listening. The first set starts a bit uneven—keyboards sit forward and guitars are low—but picks up momentum with a heartfelt Don’t Ease Me In and a superb Playing In The Band that sets the stage for an unforgettable second half. Dark Star launches into a mind‑bending jam that seamlessly morphs into a high‑energy Playing In The Band excursion, then rolls into the atmospheric Mind Left Body Jam before soaring into a beautifully plaintive Morning Dew. The encore lifts the whole concert with Eyes Of The World’s uplifting improvisation and the melancholy beauty of Stella Blue. Jerry Garcia’s tone is crisp and his solos are dexterous throughout, especially on Dark Star, MLB, Morning Dew, and Eyes Of The World; Phil Lesh provides a memorable solo in Dark Star and powers the set with bomb‑fills, while Billy Kreutzmann’s drumming is described as having “extra limbs,” driving the band forward. Despite early mix quirks, the vault‑remastered soundboard captures the performance with remarkable clarity.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All available analysis is positive, praising Jerry’s crisp tone, the stellar Dark Star → MLB jam, strong solos from Phil, and high‑energy drumming. Multiple standout songs are highlighted consistently, indicating a strongly favorable consensus across recordings.

best_recording.reason: Vault‑remastered soundboard with crisp Jerry tone, high clarity, and complete capture of the second‑set highlights.

key_highlights: Monumental Dark Star → Playing In The Band jam that defines the second set
Atmospheric Mind Left Body Jam segue into a heartfelt Morning Dew
Uplifting encore featuring Eyes Of The World and Stella Blue
Vault‑remastered SBD offers excellent clarity despite early first‑set mix quirks

band_performance.Jerry: Crisp, dexterous playing with standout solos on Dark Star, MLB, Morning Dew and Eyes Of The World.
band_performance.Phil: Great solo in Dark Star and powerful bomb‑fills that drive the second set.
band_performance.Bob: Strong vocal delivery on Sugaree; otherwise not specifically noted.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards sit forward early but balance improves later, supporting the band's flow.
band_performance.Billy: Described as having “extra limbs,” delivering high‑energy drumming throughout.
